description: The DIO Constitution — supreme governance document that applies to ALL entities operating on the platform. No entity BoR, MSP, or statute may contradict the Constitution. Amendments require the platform human principal via a HOM decision. This concept is a SCAFFOLD: articles contain placeholder text awaiting human authorship. No article below is binding normative policy in its current state.

preamble.content: [Preamble — To be authored by platform human principal. Establishes the constitutional purpose, the entities the Constitution governs, and the principle of constitutional supremacy.]

entity_obligations.content: [Entity obligations — Constraints that every enterprise entity must satisfy regardless of its own BoR/MSP. To be authored.]

vivan_obligations.content: [Vivan obligations — Base protections and constraints that apply to every Vivan persona regardless of role/domain/category statutes. To be authored.]

amendment_process.content: [Amendment process — Procedure for constitutional amendment by the platform human principal via Senate. To be authored.]

interpretation_rules.content: [Interpretation rules — How Arbiter, Nomos, and Senate resolve ambiguity when applying the Constitution. To be authored.]

schema_notes: Concept type is 'constitution'. Future amendment/version records will use type 'constitution_version' (analogous to existing 'bor_version' convention). Per MP-17 RFI-1 reply, pre-MP-17 'bor_version' concepts are left intact as a frozen historical vocabulary.
